author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses: devaloka pitṛloka⋅pāya tāra mahā⋅sukha sādhu sādhu bale anukṣaṇa jugala bhajaye jara⋅premānande bhāse tāra tāṇdera nichani tri⋅bhuvana pṛthak āyāsa⋅yoge⋅duḥkha⋅maya⋅viṣaya⋅bhoge vraje vāsa govinda⋅sevana kṛṣṇa⋅kathā kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅satya⋅satya rasa⋅dhāma vraja⋅jane⋅saṅge anukṣaṇa sadā sevā⋅abhilāṣa⋅manete kari’ viśvāsa sadā⋅kāla haiya nirbhaya narottama⋅dāsa bole paḍinu asat⋅bhole paritrāṇa kara mahāśaya
en_translation:
Those who attain the heavenly planets and the world of the forefathers find great happiness there. They always exclaim, "Excellent, excellent!" But for those who worship the Divine Couple and float in the ecstasy of Their love, the three worlds are insignificant in comparison.

Abandoning separate endeavors for sense enjoyment, which is full of misery, one should reside in Vraja and serve Govinda. Topics of Kṛṣṇa and the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa are truly, truly the abode of nectar, always to be enjoyed in the company of the residents of Vraja.

Always maintaining the desire for service and keeping faith in the mind, one becomes fearless at all times. Narottama Dāsa says, "I have fallen, bewildered by illusion. Please deliver me, O great soul."
